quality_notes: Add vulnerability: mention a mistake, failure, or lesson learned. Try 'I was wrong', 'took me years to learn', or 'finally understood'.
Remove unsupported numeric claims and metrics unless the topic explicitly provided them.
Remove unsupported hard stats (percentages, millions, salary figures) unless provided in the topic.
Add more relevant emojis to improve scanability and engagement (target: 4 to 10).
Use 4 to 7 clean hashtags at the end.
Include one fenced visual block that matches the planned diagram type.
Keep the post in the LinkedIn sweet spot: roughly 140 to 340 words.

post_text:
📌 Databricks open weight AI cost savings

I think Databricks' $188B valuation is a wake-up call for engineers to reconsider open weight AI models. Databricks has been at the forefront of this trend, publishing research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.

This approach can significantly reduce model sizes and improve inference speeds, making it a crucial consideration for engineers building systems now.

The implications of Databricks' research are far-reaching, and engineers should take note of the potential benefits of open weight AI models. As the industry continues to shift towards more efficient and cost-effective solutions, Databricks' work in this area will likely have a lasting impact.

What open weight AI models are you currently exploring in your projects, and how do you see them changing your development workflow?

#LLM #AIEngineering #GenerativeAI #MLOps #AIInProduction
